2605.07040.title: Cognitive Agent Compilation for Explicit Problem Solver Modeling
2605.07040.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are widely used for tutoring, feedback generation, and content creation, but their broad pretraining makes them hard to constrain and poor substitutes for controllable learners. Educational systems often require inspectable and editable knowledge states: educators want to know what a system assumes the learner knows, and learners benefit when the system can justify actions in terms of explicit skills, misconceptions, and strategies. Inspired by cognitive architectures, we propose Cognitive Agent Compilation (CAC), a framework that uses a strong teacher LLM to compile problem-solving knowledge into an explicit target agent. CAC separates (i) knowledge representation, (ii) problem-solving policy, and (iii) verification and update rules, with the goal of making bounded problem solving more inspectable and editable in educational settings. We present an early proof of concept implemented with Small Language Models that surfaces key design trade-offs, particularly between explicit control and scalable generalization, and positions CAC as an initial step toward bounded-knowledge AI for educational applications.
2605.07040.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）廣泛用於輔導、反饋生成和內容創建，但它們的廣泛預訓練使得約束它們變得困難，並且對於可控學習者來說是較差的替代品。教育系統通常需要可檢查和可編輯的知識狀態：教育者希望了解系統假設學習者所知道的內容，而當系統能夠根據明確的技能、誤解和策略來解釋行動時，學習者會受益。受認知架構的啟發，我們提出了認知代理編譯（CAC），這是一個框架，利用強大的教師LLM將問題解決知識編譯成明確的目標代理。CAC 分離了 (i) 知識表示、(ii) 問題解決策略和 (iii) 驗證與更新規則，目標是在教育環境中使有界問題解決更具可檢查性和可編輯性。我們展示了一個早期的概念驗證，使用小型語言模型實現，突顯了關鍵的設計權衡，特別是在明確控制和可擴展泛化之間，並將CAC定位為邁向教育應用的有界知識AI的初步步驟。
2605.07040.author: Hyeongdon Moon et.al.
2605.07040.authors: Hyeongdon Moon, Carolyn Rosé, John Stamper
